steps.title: Identify what the given equation expresses
steps.content: We are given that $m = 6(n + p)$. This equation already expresses $m$ directly in terms of the variables $n$ and $p$. The question asks us to identify which choice matches this relationship.

steps.title: Match to the correct choice
steps.content:
Comparing the given equation to the choices:
  • Choice A: $m = \frac{6(n+p)}{11}$ — introduces a division by 11 not in the given equation
  • Choice B: $m = 6(n + p)$ — exact match
  • Choice C: $m = 6(n + p) - 11$ — introduces a subtraction of 11
  • Choice D: $m = 6 - n - p - 11$ — completely different structure
The given equation matches choice B — B is correct.
